summary: Visions Of Johanna shines; strong second‑set jams
blurb: The concert opens with solid Dylan covers and a standout Visions Of Johanna, then rolls into a fiery China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider pairing. Jerry’s guitar is on fire throughout, Phil provides a blasting bass foundation, and the second set delivers powerful Playing In The Band plus an inspired Drum/Space segment. The Mighty Quinn encore caps a memorable night.
review:
March 19, 1986 at the Hampton Coliseum captured the Dead in top form. Early on, Garcia’s vocal grit and guitar finesse turned the Dylan numbers – Hell In A Bucket, Stagger Lee and C.C. Rider – into highlights, while Visions Of Johanna emerged as a centerpiece of the first set. The second half really lifts off: China Cat Sunflower segues flawlessly into I Know You Rider, then expands into a soaring Playing In The Band that builds into a tight Drum/Space excursion before melting back into The Wheel and Truckin'. Phil’s bass is “blasting hard and clean,” anchoring the band as Brent’s Hammond B‑3 organ shines on Cold Rain And Snow, and Mickey’s subtle tambourine work adds texture to Visions. Even with a few technical hiccups (a mic slip on Truckin' and some PA muffiness on the first set), the overall performance is vibrant and tightly locked in, making this show a must‑listen for anyone tracking the mid‑80s era.

For listeners seeking the clearest picture of that night, the damico soundboard capture (gd1986-03-19.nak300.damico.34223.flac16) offers a balanced mix with no dropouts and is widely regarded as the definitive version. The audience recording from Sony/Lai also delivers crystal‑clear energy if you prefer crowd ambience, while the Hessberg matrix highlights a magical Cold Rain And Snow moment. Whichever tape you spin, this Hampton show stands out for its powerful jams, inspired vocals, and an encore that leaves the crowd shouting "Quinn!".

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The majority of recordings (7/10) are positive, praising key songs and performances; only one negative and two mixed notes focus on technical issues rather than musical execution, yielding a strong overall positive sentiment.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with clear, well‑balanced mix and no dropouts; reviewers call it the definitive version.

key_highlights: Visions Of Johanna shines as a centerpiece of the first set.
Jerry’s scorching guitar on Dylan covers and China Cat > I Know You Rider.
Phil’s blasting bass provides a rock‑solid foundation throughout.
Second‑set jams – Playing In The Band, Drum/Space, and Cold Rain And Snow – are standout moments.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s vocal grit and guitar are highlighted on the Dylan covers, Visions Of Johanna, China Cat Sunflower, and Stagger Lee, described as "smoking", "attacking" and "on fire" throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Phil’s bass is praised for being "blasting hard and clean," delivering a solid, driving foundation across the entire set.
band_performance.Bob: Bob’s performance remains spirited despite a mic slip during Truckin'; his rhythm work and whammy fills on Playin' in the Band receive positive mentions.
band_performance.Brent: Brent’s Hammond B‑3 organ is clear and distinct, especially on Cold Rain And Snow, adding depth to the jam sections.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey’s drumming is powerful and steady; his subtle tambourine adds texture on Visions Of Johanna, and the Drum segment is highlighted as a standout moment.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann delivers a strong, steady drum presence that anchors the extended jams, contributing to the praised Drum/Space sequence.

must_listen_sequences: Beat It On Down The Line
